For me, that joy was \"destroying\" my online profiles and public information. Rather than just deleting them, I wanted them to disappear in a glittery poof! {"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"marks":[{"type":"italic"}],"value":"For my Introduction to Physical Computing final, I created a soft, felt interactive reproduction of Jan Steen's painting, \"As the Old Sing, So Pipe the Young\". ","nodeType":"text"}],"nodeType":"paragraph"},{"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"marks":[{"type":"italic"}],"value":"Steen visualizes the Dutch proverb (a.k.a the title), which means bad example leads to bad conduct through the scene of a lively baby's baptism. Rife with symbolism and dynamic movement, this painting's symbolic elements (the footwarmer, a blue hat, a pipe, and a dog) are highlighted by three liftable flaps and raised surfaces, which reveal informative text on an adjacent screen.","nodeType":"text"}],"nodeType":"paragraph"},{"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"marks":[{"type":"italic"}],"value":"My objective was to create a fun introduction to a subject--specifically 17th century Dutch Baroque paintings--that may seem initially uninteresting or intimidating!","nodeType":"text"}],"nodeType":"paragraph"},{"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"marks":[{"type":"italic"}],"value":"This project utilizes an ","nodeType":"text"},{"data":{},"marks":[{"type":"bold"},{"type":"italic"}],"value":"Arduino UNO,  3 photoresistors, 1 FSR, 1 servo motor ","nodeType":"text"},{"data":{},"marks":[{"type":"italic"}],"value":"and ","nodeType":"text"},{"data":{},"marks":[{"type":"bold"},{"type":"italic"}],"value":"p5.js","nodeType":"text"},{"data":{},"marks":[{"type":"italic"}],"value":".","nodeType":"text"}],"nodeType":"paragraph"},{"data":{},"content":[],"nodeType":"hr"},{"data":{"target":{"sys":{"id":"c4045fJJbLuA1KRhTVzHYaK","type":"Link","linkType":"Asset","contentful_id":"4045fJJbLuA1KRhTVzHYaK"}}},"content":[],"nodeType":"embedded-asset-block"},{"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"marks":[],"value":"Ideation ","nodeType":"text"}],"nodeType":"heading-2"},{"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"marks":[],"value":"Despite being an art history major, I dreaded taking courses examining anytime prior to the 19th century. However, these classes became the most memorable as they enchanted me with rich narratives and dramaticism that rendered me emotional. My desire to share this excitement towards the subject led me to the idea of an interactive physical painting.","nodeType":"text"}],"nodeType":"paragraph"},{"data":{"target":{"sys":{"id":"c6YgdqyzMQa4nVdi3aXcAfY","type":"Link","linkType":"Asset","contentful_id":"6YgdqyzMQa4nVdi3aXcAfY"}}},"content":[],"nodeType":"embedded-asset-block"},{"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"marks":[],"value":"Prototype","nodeType":"text"}],"nodeType":"heading-2"},{"data":{"target":{"sys":{"id":"j6NzYMs490kxZVvZWvux4","type":"Link","linkType":"Asset","contentful_id":"j6NzYMs490kxZVvZWvux4"}}},"content":[],"nodeType":"embedded-asset-block"},{"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"marks":[],"value":"These were the guiding themes I wanted to highlight: ","nodeType":"text"}],"nodeType":"paragraph"},{"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"value":"Soft & Touchable","nodeType":"text"}],"nodeType":"paragraph"},{"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"marks":[{"type":"italic"}],"value":"Most of the time, someone's first encounter with these types of paintings are within a museum. Thus, most of their interactions with the work are confined to observing and reading, which for some can be constraining! A soft material would invite touch and remove the distance between the viewer and artwork.","nodeType":"text"}],"nodeType":"paragraph"},{"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"value":"Educational","nodeType":"text"}],"nodeType":"paragraph"},{"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"marks":[{"type":"italic"}],"value":"Initially, I gave myself the choice between the flaps triggering sounds or conjuring informative text. I chose to have informative text displayed on a screen separate from the painting to mimic way paintings are displayed in a museum and have users walk away with knowledge plus the experience!","nodeType":"text"}],"nodeType":"paragraph"},{"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"value":"A Loose Representation","nodeType":"text"}],"nodeType":"paragraph"},{"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"marks":[{"type":"italic"}],"value":"As I wanted this painting to be casual and introductory, I decided visually it would be a loose and less detailed interpretation of the original.","nodeType":"text"}],"nodeType":"paragraph"},{"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"marks":[],"value":"Below shows a deconstructed version of the physical painting, which has the electronics in a box and a needle-felted painting and felt frame on top. ","nodeType":"text"}],"nodeType":"paragraph"},{"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"marks":[],"value":"","nodeType":"text"}],"nodeType":"paragraph"},{"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"marks":[],"value":"Final","nodeType":"text"}],"nodeType":"heading-2"},{"data":{"target":{"sys":{"id":"c4wOwLATrQcHFlwhPPCzpDR","type":"Link","linkType":"Asset","contentful_id":"4wOwLATrQcHFlwhPPCzpDR"}}},"content":[],"nodeType":"embedded-asset-block"},{"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"marks":[],"value":"The soft painting contains three flaps with triangular bits of velcro at the ends that represent the dog, pipe, and footwarmer. Underneath the flaps are photoresistors which detect whether it is exposed to light or not. When it is covered, the photoresistor sends serially to p5 to trigger the relevant informative text on the iPad screen. When all 3 flaps are covered, the woman's arm (servo motor) is triggered to lift for more wine and the iPad screen reveals a congratulatory message. The dog, which has a raised surface, has a force sensitive resistor underneath that is also triggers an informative when pressed down on.","nodeType":"text"}],"nodeType":"paragraph"},{"data":{"target":{"sys":{"id":"c7ASM2HraZmVjlt2PXsHb1b","type":"Link","linkType":"Asset","contentful_id":"7ASM2HraZmVjlt2PXsHb1b"}}},"content":[],"nodeType":"embedded-asset-block"},{"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"marks":[],"value":"","nodeType":"text"}],"nodeType":"paragraph"},{"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"marks":[],"value":"","nodeType":"text"}],"nodeType":"paragraph"},{"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"marks":[],"value":"","nodeType":"text"}],"nodeType":"paragraph"}],"nodeType":"document"}

{"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"marks":[{"type":"italic"}],"value":"Sing along in this","nodeType":"text"},{"data":{},"marks":[{"type":"bold"},{"type":"italic"}],"value":" ","nodeType":"text"},{"data":{"uri":"https://editor.p5js.org/full/HyC9c_Zhm"},"content":[{"data":{},"marks":[{"type":"bold"},{"type":"italic"}],"value":"p5 sketch of Silly Duet","nodeType":"text"}],"nodeType":"hyperlink"},{"data":{},"marks":[{"type":"italic"}],"value":"!","nodeType":"text"}],"nodeType":"paragraph"},{"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"marks":[{"type":"italic"}],"value":"This is for all the times I've ever wanted to shout \"STUPID\" out of frustration at myself, my computer, or every other happening in my life. ","nodeType":"text"}],"nodeType":"paragraph"},{"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"marks":[{"type":"italic"}],"value":"You just have to say \"you are stupid\" and the computer will respond and vent along to tune. ","nodeType":"text"},{"data":{"uri":"https://p5js.org/"},"content":[{"data":{},"marks":[{"type":"italic"}],"value":"p5.js ","nodeType":"text"}],"nodeType":"hyperlink"},{"data":{},"marks":[{"type":"italic"}],"value":"is an open source javascript library for creative coding started with the express goal of ‘making coding accessible for artists, designers, educators, and beginners.’ For the summer, I worked with Cassie Tarakajian to make a search bar for the ","nodeType":"text"},{"data":{"uri":"https://editor.p5js.org/"},"content":[{"data":{},"marks":[{"type":"italic"}],"value":"p5.js Web Editor!","nodeType":"text"}],"nodeType":"hyperlink"},{"data":{},"marks":[{"type":"italic"}],"value":"","nodeType":"text"}],"nodeType":"paragraph"},{"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"marks":[{"type":"italic"}],"value":"This ","nodeType":"text"},{"data":{"uri":"https://medium.com/processing-foundation/search-bar-for-sketches-in-the-p5-js-web-editor-e6e9cda3909d"},"content":[{"data":{},"marks":[{"type":"italic"}],"value":"Medium article","nodeType":"text"}],"nodeType":"hyperlink"},{"data":{},"marks":[{"type":"italic"}],"value":" also summarizes what I've done! ","nodeType":"text"}],"nodeType":"paragraph"},{"data":{},"content":[],"nodeType":"hr"},{"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"marks":[],"value":"Before this project, I had never been involved with open source or programmed outside of a classroom setting. I began the summer learning best contribution practices, how the web editor is structured, and why it was organized that way. Afterwards, I was introduced to ","nodeType":"text"},{"data":{"uri":"https://git-scm.com/"},"content":[{"data":{},"marks":[],"value":"Git","nodeType":"text"}],"nodeType":"hyperlink"},{"data":{},"marks":[],"value":", ","nodeType":"text"},{"data":{"uri":"https://reactjs.org/"},"content":[{"data":{},"marks":[],"value":"React","nodeType":"text"}],"nodeType":"hyperlink"},{"data":{},"marks":[],"value":", and ","nodeType":"text"},{"data":{"uri":"https://sass-lang.com/"},"content":[{"data":{},"marks":[],"value":"Sass","nodeType":"text"}],"nodeType":"hyperlink"},{"data":{},"marks":[],"value":".","nodeType":"text"}],"nodeType":"paragraph"},{"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"marks":[],"value":" I worked with two issues (","nodeType":"text"},{"data":{"uri":"https://github.com/processing/p5.js-web-editor/issues/989"},"content":[{"data":{},"marks":[],"value":"#989","nodeType":"text"}],"nodeType":"hyperlink"},{"data":{},"marks":[],"value":", ","nodeType":"text"},{"data":{"uri":"https://github.com/processing/p5.js-web-editor/pull/1109#pullrequestreview-253008128"},"content":[{"data":{},"marks":[],"value":"#1109","nodeType":"text"}],"nodeType":"hyperlink"},{"data":{},"marks":[],"value":") to learn how to make visual and functional changes within the web editor. The first issue had a design that led to questions about its intention and implementation within the code, and was put on hold for further discussion. Although it was unable to be resolved, I learned that implementing design through code was not always straightforward; frequent communication between the programmer and designer is very pivotal to the process.","nodeType":"text"}],"nodeType":"paragraph"},{"data":{"target":{"sys":{"space":{"sys":{"type":"Link","linkType":"Space","id":"xrbc1asbsx39","contentful_id":"xrbc1asbsx39"}},"id":"c6JI9BDB5vQYUOxlqdhyMh2","type":"Asset","createdAt":"2020-03-17T00:45:25.080Z","updatedAt":"2020-03-17T00:45:25.080Z","environment":{"sys":{"id":"master","type":"Link","linkType":"Environment","contentful_id":"master"}},"revision":1,"contentful_id":"6JI9BDB5vQYUOxlqdhyMh2"},"fields":{"title":{"en-US":"hearthstone"},"file":{"en-US":{"url":"//images.ctfassets.net/xrbc1asbsx39/6JI9BDB5vQYUOxlqdhyMh2/dd4adf44c74be4d76105657ae29d0ccc/hearthstone.gif","details":{"size":1573455,"image":{"width":600,"height":411}},"fileName":"hearthstone.gif","contentType":"image/gif"}}}}},"content":[],"nodeType":"embedded-asset-block"},{"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"marks":[],"value":"I created a portfolio site with pop-up modals to understand React components and how data is being passed between them. I also made a mock search bar that searched within a list of ","nodeType":"text"},{"data":{"uri":"https://hearthstonejson.com/"},"content":[{"data":{},"marks":[],"value":"Hearthstone cards","nodeType":"text"}],"nodeType":"hyperlink"},{"data":{},"marks":[],"value":". Assembling these examples was extremely helpful in learning these libraries and repeating the same process for the web editor.","nodeType":"text"}],"nodeType":"paragraph"},{"data":{"target":{"sys":{"space":{"sys":{"type":"Link","linkType":"Space","id":"xrbc1asbsx39","contentful_id":"xrbc1asbsx39"}},"id":"c16NVgNdihG0l9ZxgCy9gdI","type":"Asset","createdAt":"2020-03-17T00:45:42.437Z","updatedAt":"2020-03-17T00:45:42.437Z","environment":{"sys":{"id":"master","type":"Link","linkType":"Environment","contentful_id":"master"}},"revision":1,"contentful_id":"16NVgNdihG0l9ZxgCy9gdI"},"fields":{"title":{"en-US":"zeplin"},"file":{"en-US":{"url":"//images.ctfassets.net/xrbc1asbsx39/16NVgNdihG0l9ZxgCy9gdI/24c0367237be950fdba63c675f7b833a/zeplin.png","details":{"size":79960,"image":{"width":1308,"height":796}},"fileName":"zeplin.png","contentType":"image/png"}}}}},"content":[],"nodeType":"embedded-asset-block"},{"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"marks":[],"value":"I used designs uploaded to Zeplin as visual guides for how the search bar would appear in its ","nodeType":"text"},{"data":{"uri":"https://app.zeplin.io/project/55f746c54a02e1e50e0632c3/screen/59413d89c2b5318d69be12d3"},"content":[{"data":{},"marks":[],"value":"neutral","nodeType":"text"}],"nodeType":"hyperlink"},{"data":{},"marks":[],"value":" and ","nodeType":"text"},{"data":{"uri":"https://app.zeplin.io/project/55f746c54a02e1e50e0632c3/screen/59413d88cda26c1669f83fea"},"content":[{"data":{},"marks":[],"value":"active","nodeType":"text"}],"nodeType":"hyperlink"},{"data":{},"marks":[],"value":" state. I ran into some questions involving other aspects of the designs, which showed the search icon as a toggle for hiding and viewing the search bar. I was also unsure as to whether a search button would also be necessary as the results would be presented live. I turned to some resources on","nodeType":"text"},{"data":{"uri":"https://uxplanet.org/design-a-perfect-search-box-b6baaf9599c"},"content":[{"data":{},"marks":[],"value":" best practices for a search bar","nodeType":"text"}],"nodeType":"hyperlink"},{"data":{},"marks":[],"value":" to finalize decisions, which advised providing prominent access, a search button, and a magnifying-glass icon.","nodeType":"text"}],"nodeType":"paragraph"},{"data":{"target":{"sys":{"space":{"sys":{"type":"Link","linkType":"Space","id":"xrbc1asbsx39","contentful_id":"xrbc1asbsx39"}},"id":"c3koYM1y9Iws4yEy8zSgaJQ","type":"Asset","createdAt":"2020-03-17T00:46:00.569Z","updatedAt":"2020-03-17T00:46:00.569Z","environment":{"sys":{"id":"master","type":"Link","linkType":"Environment","contentful_id":"master"}},"revision":1,"contentful_id":"3koYM1y9Iws4yEy8zSgaJQ"},"fields":{"title":{"en-US":"search"},"file":{"en-US":{"url":"//images.ctfassets.net/xrbc1asbsx39/3koYM1y9Iws4yEy8zSgaJQ/639574a266f3b66c9a1ec2bf5dd5d16a/search.gif","details":{"size":315219,"image":{"width":600,"height":491}},"fileName":"search.gif","contentType":"image/gif"}}}}},"content":[],"nodeType":"embedded-asset-block"},{"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"marks":[],"value":"The search bar, placed within the modal that displays a list of the user’s sketches, has a search icon that serves as a search button, a text input area, and clear button. The color scheme of the buttons and border are dependent on the theme","nodeType":"text"}],"nodeType":"paragraph"},{"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"marks":[],"value":"","nodeType":"text"},{"data":{"uri":"https://camo.githubusercontent.com/95908d5c9ee72f8b77ab0cc701ce227effafbc2f/68747470733a2f2f64726976652e676f6f676c652e636f6d2f75633f6578706f72743d766965772669643d314b4352463636716b54556e7346655f64767a4f32464e7168396a6c377a4a364d"},"content":[{"data":{},"marks":[],"value":"","nodeType":"text"}],"nodeType":"hyperlink"},{"data":{},"marks":[],"value":"The search bar is live, which means that the sketchlist is filtered as the user types. To prevent the list from getting filtered after every keystroke, this filter function was throttled, or arranged to happen periodically after every set time frame. The search icon button will also call the filtering to happen if pressed. If the user wishes to clear their query, the clear button will reset it to an empty string. The search query also gets refreshed whenever the sketch list overlay is re-opened.","nodeType":"text"}],"nodeType":"paragraph"},{"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"marks":[{"type":"italic"}],"value":"I deeply express my gratitude to the Processing foundation and Google Summer of Code for giving me such a wonderful opportunity and to my mentor, Cassie Tarakajian, for her education, patience, and support!","nodeType":"text"}],"nodeType":"paragraph"}],"nodeType":"document"}
